message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_cross autograd via apply_function with bilinear backward [bd-j2rj]

tensor_cross used to extract values, compute the cross product
elementwise, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — same
severed-autograd pattern fixed under frankentorch-1tax.

Cross product is bilinear, so the JVPs are themselves cross
products:
    grad_a = b × grad_c
    grad_b = grad_c × a

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this analytical backward.
Save a and b for backward (both 3-vectors so cost is constant).

Adds cross_propagates_gradient_through_a_and_b regression test:
a = e_x = [1,0,0], b = e_y = [0,1,0] → c = e_z = [0,0,1]; under
sum loss with grad_c = [1,1,1] the analytical gradients are
grad_a = [1, 0, -1], grad_b = [0, 1, -1] (asserts to 1e-12).

Closes frankentorch-j2rj. ft-api lib tests: 1144 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -104,6 +104,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-iha","title":"Add mixed precision support (torch.amp equivalent)","description":"torch.cuda.amp (Automatic Mixed Precision) is used in virtually every modern training pipeline. Even on CPU, F16/BF16 mixed precision can be useful for inference and for testing AMP workflows before moving to GPU.

Since FrankenTorch already supports F16 and BF16 dtypes in ft-core, the infrastructure exists — what's missing is the autocast and grad scaling machinery.

Implementation plan:
1. Add an AutocastContext to ft-api that tracks whether autocast is active and the target dtype
2. Add session.autocast_enter(dtype) / session.autocast_exit() (matching no_grad pattern)
3. Add session.with_autocast(dtype, closure) panic-safe wrapper
4. When autocast is active: matmul, linear, conv operations auto-downcast inputs to the target dtype (F16/BF16), then upcast output back to F32
5. Add a GradScaler struct in ft-optim that:
   a. Scales loss by a factor before backward (prevents F16 underflow)
   b. Unscales gradients before optimizer step
   c. Checks for inf/nan in gradients, skipping the update if found
   d. Adjusts scale factor dynamically based on overflow frequency

Key: The autocast policy (which ops to cast) should match PyTorch's known-safe list — matmul, linear, conv are safe; softmax, layernorm, loss functions should stay in F32.

Tests:
- Unit test: autocast context enters/exits correctly, respects nesting
- Unit test: matmul inside autocast uses F16, outside uses F32
- Unit test: GradScaler scales loss and unscales gradients
- Unit test: GradScaler skips update on inf gradients
- E2E test: train a linear model with autocast + GradScaler, verify convergence
- Panic-safety: with_autocast restores state on unwind","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:09.970716552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450624484Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450297625Z","close_reason":"Implemented mixed precision support: (1) ft-api: autocast_enter/exit/with_autocast/maybe_autocast with stack-based RAII context, panic-safe; (2) ft-optim: GradScaler with scale_loss/step/update + dynamic backoff/growth, overflow detection, skip-on-inf semantics; (3) ft-autograd: TensorBackwardReport::tensor_gradients_iter() and scaled_clone() helpers. 8 autocast tests + 7 GradScaler tests = 15 new tests. Note: F16/BF16 conversion paths gated until ft-autograd to_dtype supports them; F32/F64 down-casting works.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iiv","title":"Reject impossible multinomial draws without replacement","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:30:49.283027420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T02:34:11.910610518Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T02:34:11.910332993Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ioo","title":"Add CI pipeline with G1-G5 quality gates from spec","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §18 defines 5 CI gates that are release-blocking. NONE of them exist:

| Gate | Name | Blocking | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G1 | format + lint | yes | lint report |
| G2 | unit + integration | yes | junit report |
| G3 | differential + gradient checks | yes | parity report + grad report |
| G4 | adversarial + property tests | yes | counterexample corpus |
| G5 | benchmark regression | yes | baseline delta report |

Implementation:
1. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with 5 jobs mapping to G1-G5
2. G1: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings
3. G2: cargo test --workspace --all-features (with junit output via cargo-nextest or similar)
4. G3: cargo test -p ft-conformance (differential fixtures), cargo test -p ft-autograd (gradient checks)
5. G4: cargo test --workspace --all-features (proptest runs as part of normal test suite)
6. G5: cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos (depends on frankentorch-vws being done first)

Considerations:
- GitHub Actions runners may be slow for full test suite; consider splitting into parallel jobs
- G5 (benchmark regression) needs baseline artifacts committed to repo for comparison
- Need to use nightly toolchain (rust-toolchain.toml already specifies this)

Tests:
- The CI pipeline IS the test — verify it by pushing a PR and checking all 5 gates pass
- Ensure the pipeline also runs on the 'main' branch (not just PRs)
- Add a badge to README.md showing CI status","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:38.761474655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:43:08.208191542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:43:08.207936639Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: .github/workflows/phase2c_reliability_gates.yml has G1 (fmt/clippy) + G2 (unit/property) + G3 (differential) + G4 (e2e/forensics) + G5 (perf) + G6 (schema) + G7 (RaptorQ) + G8 (readiness). Exceeds spec's G1-G5 requirement. Uses rch offload when available, falls back to local. Has structured failure reporting with gate_id, replay commands, artifact refs, and reason codes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-3cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.351795619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankentorch-ioo","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-vws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.900747037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankentorch-j2rj","title":"tensor_cross is severed-autograd: bilinear backward, easy fix","description":"tensor_cross at lib.rs:3354 extracts a_values + b_values, computes the cross product elementwise, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.cross is differentiable.

Cross product is bilinear:
   c = a × b   where c_0 = a_1 b_2 - a_2 b_1, c_1 = a_2 b_0 - a_0 b_2, c_2 = a_0 b_1 - a_1 b_0

Under chain rule with upstream grad_c:
   grad_a = b × grad_c
   grad_b = grad_c × a

Equivalent (and easier to implement) componentwise:
   grad_a_i = sum_j (∂c_j/∂a_i) * grad_c_j
The Jacobian is 3×3 and antisymmetric in a swap of (a, b).

Fix: wrap in tensor_apply_function with the bilinear backward. Inputs are [3]-vectors so cost is constant per call. Save a and b for backward.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass scanning for the values-then-non-grad-leaf pattern after closing tw0y.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cross in tensor_apply_function with the bilinear backward grad_a = b × grad_c, grad_b = grad_c × a. Both inputs are saved for backward (each is a 3-vector). Added cross_propagates_gradient_through_a_and_b regression test (a = e_x, b = e_y, c = a × b = e_z; sum loss → grad_a = [1, 0, -1], grad_b = [0, 1, -1], asserts to 1e-12). ft-api lib tests: 1144.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:15:46.878708994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:17:35.882615282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:17:35.879838189Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-j33","title":"ft-core: fail closed on integer/bool storage span overflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:33.514330249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T16:21:38.818312755Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T16:21:38.818066108Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-jhi","title":"Make Optimizer::set_momentum return Result instead of silent no-op","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that `Optimizer::set_momentum(&mut self, _momentum: f64)` in ft-optim/src/lib.rs:111 has an empty body `{}`. This is the ONLY empty function body in the entire codebase.

The paired `get_momentum()` default returns `None`, which is correct. But `set_momentum()` silently discards the value with no feedback — a caller setting momentum on an Adagrad optimizer (which does not support momentum) gets no error and no indication that their intent was ignored.

## Problem
Silent data loss violates the principle of least surprise. In PyTorch, calling an unsupported setter is either a no-op with a warning or raises an error depending on the optimizer. FrankenTorch should be at least as explicit.

## Plan
1. Change signature to `fn set_momentum(&mut self, _momentum: f64) -> Result<(), AutogradError>`
2. Default implementation returns `Err(optimizer_hparam_error("this optimizer does not support momentum"))`
3. SGD (and any other momentum-supporting optimizer) overrides to actually set the value and return `Ok(())`
4. Update all callers to handle the Result
5. Add a test verifying that non-momentum optimizers reject set_momentum

## Acceptance Criteria
- `set_momentum` on Adagrad/RMSprop/RAdam returns descriptive Err
- `set_momentum` on SGD succeeds
- All existing optimizer tests pass
- Zero empty function bodies remain in the codebase

## Dependencies
None — this is an independent API change within ft-optim.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:22:34.711976324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:45:29.961692699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:45:29.961433909Z","close_reason":"Optimizer::set_momentum now returns an explicit unsupported-feature error by default, SGD implements Result-based momentum updates, OneCycleLR handles failures visibly, and ft-optim tests plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Prevents panic on NaN eigenvalues/singular values.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Added tril_indices and triu_indices with offset support. 4 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-plj","title":"Add gradient checkpointing (torch.utils.checkpoint equivalent)","description":"torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() allows trading compute for memory by not saving intermediate activations during forward, then recomputing them during backward. This is essential for training large models that don't fit in memory.

Implementation plan:
1. Add a session.checkpoint(function, inputs) method that:
   a. Runs the function in no_grad context (saves no intermediates)
   b. Records a special CheckpointedSegment node on the autograd tape
   c. During backward: re-runs the function WITH grad enabled to recompute activations, then computes gradients normally
2. The key invariant: gradients must be identical whether checkpoint is used or not (just slower with lower memory)

Design considerations:
- Must work with the existing TensorTape backward scheduler
- Must properly handle nested checkpointing
- Must respect the DAC contract (deterministic replay must still work)
- Re-execution during backward must produce identical activations (deterministic PRNG state needs saving)

Tests:
- Unit test: simple function with checkpoint vs without — gradients must match exactly
- Unit test: nested checkpointing (checkpoint inside checkpoint)
- Unit test: checkpoint respects manual_seed for reproducibility
- E2E test: train a deep network with and without checkpointing — final loss should be identical
- Memory test: verify peak memory is lower with checkpointing (if measurable)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:52.757885399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:14.882158581Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:14.881802397Z","close_reason":"Implemented session.checkpoint() that trades compute for memory. Runs function in no_grad (no intermediates saved), registers backward node via tensor_apply_function that re-executes the function with grad enabled during backward. 3 tests: correct forward output, gradient equivalence with non-checkpointed, multiple inputs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ps7","title":"Implement tensor_vander (Vandermonde matrix)","description":"Add torch.vander for polynomial fitting and signal processing.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:14:34.983231294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:45:47.810840473Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:45:47.810526477Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_vander with increasing/decreasing modes and custom N. 3 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-pvfk","title":"tensor_linalg_det / slogdet return raw f64 instead of TensorNodeId — breaks autograd","description":"tensor_linalg_det at lib.rs:12132 returns Result<f64, AutogradError> and tensor_linalg_slogdet at lib.rs:12146 returns Result<(f64, f64), AutogradError>. PyTorch's torch.linalg.det / torch.linalg.slogdet return 0-D tensors that participate in the autograd graph.

The current API: callers extract raw f64 → can't differentiate, can't compose with downstream tensor ops, can't carry through training loops.

Fix is a breaking API change but justified for parity:
  - tensor_linalg_det → Result<TensorNodeId, AutogradError>
    backward: grad_A = grad_out * det(A) * (A^{-1})^T = grad_out * Y * (A^{-1})^T
    Save A^{-1} (or A and recompute) for backward.
  - tensor_linalg_slogdet → Result<(TensorNodeId, TensorNodeId), AutogradError>
    sign is non-differentiable (constant in {-1, 0, 1}); logabsdet has
    backward grad_A = grad_logabsdet * (A^{-1})^T (Jacobi formula).

Migration path: add new tensor_linalg_det_node / tensor_linalg_slogdet_node returning TensorNodeId, deprecate the f64-returning forms with a documented removal target. Or change directly if the breaking change is acceptable.

Surfaced during /reality-check pass following the linalg severed-autograd cleanup (frankentorch-tw0y children: linalg_inv, triangular_solve, cholesky_solve).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:13:07.838801729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:13:07.838801729Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-qbyz","title":"ft-nn Fold autograd composition fails gradient propagation","description":"Reviewer finding from 2026-04-29. In the current ft-nn worktree, running CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_frankentorch_cod2_reviewer RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_VISIBILITY=summary rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn fails tests::fold_propagates_gradients. Reproduction failure: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs:17754 unwraps Err TensorRootDoesNotRequireGrad { node: TensorNodeId(6) }. Context: recent in-progress Fold/Unfold edits compose Fold through tensor_scatter_add over a zeros root, but Fold's output root is not considered requiring grad, so backward cannot recover the input gradient. This is high severity because Fold has forward-correct behavior but silently breaks autograd, violating the deterministic autograd correctness contract.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:05:57.829684923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T12:32:16.063639293Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T12:32:16.061912455Z","close_reason":"Fixed by extending ft-autograd ScatterAdd to track src node and gather incoming gradient at index positions in backward; ft-api tensor_scatter_add now passes src node down to autograd. Fold composition through tensor_scatter_add now propagates gradients correctly. fold_propagates_gradients passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-qcs","title":"Add integration test verifying zero panic/todo/unimplemented in production code","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) was a manual multi-agent process. To prevent regressions, we need an automated gate that catches newly-introduced stubs, placeholders, and panic-inducing macros in production code.

All prerequisite beads completed: dwy (storage panic), jhi (set_momentum), aqm (DType exhaustiveness), aca (unreachable messages), tk5 (module error enrichment).

## Goal
Create a source-scanning integration test in ft-conformance that fails if prohibited patterns appear in production Rust source code.

## Prohibited patterns (MUST cause test failure in production code)
1. `todo!()` macro
2. `unimplemented!()` macro
3. `panic!(` — bare panic calls (NOT assert!/debug_assert! which are different macros)

## Allowed patterns (MUST NOT cause false positives)
1. Any pattern inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` blocks — tests can panic freely
2. `unreachable!("...")\ — with a message string (legitimate defensive assertions)
3. `assert!()`, `assert_eq!()`, `debug_assert!()` — NOT panic calls
4. String literals that happen to contain prohibited words (e.g., test case names like "panic!")
5. Doc comments that use words like "placeholder" in their technical meaning (e.g., nn.Identity's "Useful as a placeholder in Sequential containers")

## Known legitimate use of 'placeholder' word
The Identity module doc in ft-nn/src/lib.rs says: "Useful as a placeholder in Sequential containers or for skip connections". This is a technical description of nn.Identity's PURPOSE, not a code-incompleteness marker. The stub-marker test must NOT flag doc-comment uses of these words when they appear inside `///` or `//!` doc comments.

## Implementation approach: scan-up-to-cfg-test
Use the simple approach: for each .rs file, find the line number of `#[cfg(test)]` and only scan lines BEFORE that marker. This is correct for this codebase because:
- Every crate has exactly one `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block at the bottom
- No test code appears above this marker
- Binary crates (ft-conformance/src/bin/*.rs) have NO test blocks — scan the entire file

For files WITHOUT a #[cfg(test)] marker, scan the entire file.

## Test file location
`crates/ft-conformance/tests/stub_audit.rs` — standalone integration test.

## Test functions to implement (5 total)

### 1. test_no_todo_in_production
Scan all .rs files in crates/ (recursively). For each file, scan only production lines (before #[cfg(test)]). Assert zero lines matching the regex `\btodo!\(`. Print violations as `VIOLATION: {path}:{line_num}: {trimmed_line}`.

### 2. test_no_unimplemented_in_production
Same approach for `\bunimplemented!\(`.

### 3. test_no_bare_panic_in_production
Scan production lines for `panic!(\(`. EXCLUDE lines that:
- Are inside string literals (line starts with `"` after trimming, or contains `=>" panic")
- Contain "panic!" as a string literal (e.g., in test fixture data like smoke.rs line 156)
To avoid complex string-literal parsing, use a simple heuristic: skip lines where `panic!` appears after a `"` character that is not preceded by a backslash.

### 4. test_no_bare_unreachable_without_message
Scan production lines for bare `unreachable!()` (with empty parens — no message). Assert zero matches. Lines with `unreachable!("...")\ are fine.

### 5. test_no_stub_markers_in_production_comments
Scan production lines for TODO, FIXME, HACK, STUB (case-insensitive) in comments. A "comment" line is one where the pattern appears after `//` (line comment) or inside `/* */` (block comment). EXCLUDE matches inside doc comments (`///` and `//!`) since those may legitimately use words like "placeholder" in technical descriptions.

## Logging requirements
Each test MUST use `eprintln!` for structured output:
- On start: `[stub_audit] Scanning {N} files for {pattern_name}...`
- Per violation: `[stub_audit] VIOLATION {path}:{line}: {pattern}: {content}`
- On finish: `[stub_audit] {pattern_name}: {violations} violations in {files} files ({prod_lines} production lines)`

## Testing the tests themselves
After implementing, verify:
1. All 5 tests pass on current codebase: `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-conformance --test stub_audit -- --nocapture`
2. Inject `todo!()` into ft-core/src/lib.rs line 2, run tests, confirm test_no_todo_in_production FAILS
3. Remove the injected todo!(), confirm tests pass again
4. Verify the Identity module "placeholder" doc comment does NOT trigger test_no_stub_markers

## Acceptance Criteria
- 5 test functions in stub_audit.rs, all passing
- Each test has structured logging
- Zero false positives on current codebase
- Confirmed that injecting prohibited patterns causes test failure
- Clippy clean on the new test file

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: frankentorch-dwy (CLOSED), frankentorch-aca (CLOSED) — both resolved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:46.486851149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:11:44.707272087Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:11:44.707100282Z","close_reason":"Added ft-conformance regression gate that scans production Rust sources for todo!/unimplemented!/panic! while skipping test-only code, and verified the new smoke test plus workspace check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-qcs","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-aca","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:24:13.807546637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankentorch-qcs","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-dwy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:24:13.730286250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-qj6","title":"Implement FocalLoss in ft-nn","description":"Add FocalLoss for class-imbalanced classification. Critical for object detection (YOLO, RetinaNet). Formula: -alpha_t * (1-p_t)^gamma * log(p_t). Parameters: alpha, gamma (default 2.0), reduction.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:33:20.233703803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:40:35.139126708Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:40:35.138872938Z","close_reason":"Implemented FocalLoss with alpha, gamma, Reduction modes. 3 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
